# @epure/vitest

@epure/vitest is a Vite plugin that translates Gherkin feature files and
Gherkin blocks in Markdown into Vitest suites in memory. It also provides
ReScript bindings for Vitest.

Documentation: https://epurejs.dev
Repository: https://github.com/epuremethod/vitest
TypeScript contract: https://github.com/epuremethod/vitest/blob/main/epure-vitest/src/index.ts
ReScript contract: https://github.com/epuremethod/vitest/blob/main/epure-vitest/src/EpureVitest.resi

Installed contracts:
- dist/index.d.ts
- src/EpureVitest.resi

## Mental model

- `epureVitest()` connects contract files to Vitest through Vite.
- `Given` registers the builder for a scenario's initial step.
- The builder receives named step functions. Operations registered through
  those functions close over the state created by that builder.
- Each scenario gets its own state and can run concurrently.
- `toRecords`, `toStrings`, and `toNumbers` convert Gherkin data tables.

## Critical rules

- Include contract extensions such as `.feature` and `.md` in Vitest's
  `test.include`.
- Place the steps module beside its contract using one of the documented
  resolver conventions, or provide `stepsResolver`.
- Use `EpureVitest` from ReScript. `VitestBdd` is a deprecated compatibility
  module.
- Use `epureVitest` and `EpureVitestOptions` in TypeScript. Their `vitestBdd`
  and `VitestBddOptions` aliases are deprecated.

## Wrong analogies

- There is no Cucumber World or Cucumber hooks registry. Vitest lifecycle hooks
  remain available.
- This is not a second test runner. Vitest runs the translated suites.
- A contract is not converted into a generated test file on disk.

## Semantic traps

- Vite does perform an in-memory translation from a feature or Markdown
  contract to JavaScript.
- Step matching supports normalized placeholders such as `{string}` and
  `{number}`. It is not a complete Cucumber Expressions implementation.
- Scenario operations are available only after their matching `Given` builder
  has registered them.

## Deliberate non-goals

- Cucumber World compatibility.
- A global step registry shared across files.
- A complete Cucumber Expressions implementation.
- Generated test files committed to the repository.
